16S SOD Experiments

2024-05-21

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/Documents/Postdoc/SOD_Microbiome")
library(phyloseq); packageVersion("phyloseq")
## [1] '1.50.0'
library(ggplot2); packageVersion("ggplot2")
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.4.3
## [1] '4.0.2'
library(readr); packageVersion("readr")
## [1] '2.1.5'

## [1] '1.2.0'

theme_set(theme_cowplot())

Load data

load("Data/16S/filtered_data/sodexp.RData")
load("Data/16S/filtered_data/sod_5asv.RData")

sod.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, 
                                              levels = c("untreated","SODtrt","wildfire"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated","Treated","Wildfire"))

sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, 
                                              levels = c("untreated","SODtrt","wildfire"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated","Treated","Wildfire"))


filtered_read_counts<-read.csv("Data/16S/filtered_data/16S_subset_track_reads_sod.csv")
summary(filtered_read_counts)
##       X                 input          filtered       denoisedF    
##  Length:98          Min.   :16297   Min.   :12561   Min.   :11069  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:28042   1st Qu.:22272   1st Qu.:19990  
##  Mode  :character   Median :34454   Median :26962   Median :24454  
##                     Mean   :36682   Mean   :28478   Mean   :26069  
##                     3rd Qu.:42840   3rd Qu.:33323   3rd Qu.:30554  
##                     Max.   :91755   Max.   :70974   Max.   :65545  
##    denoisedR         merged         nonchim     
##  Min.   :10734   Min.   : 8050   Min.   : 8029  
##  1st Qu.:19866   1st Qu.:15877   1st Qu.:15635  
##  Median :24366   Median :20056   Median :19814  
##  Mean   :25988   Mean   :21519   Mean   :21114  
##  3rd Qu.:30522   3rd Qu.:25226   3rd Qu.:25044  
##  Max.   :65421   Max.   :55313   Max.   :54662
new_names <- sapply(strsplit(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_name16S, "-"), function(x) {
  substr(x[1], 3, nchar(x[1]))
})

sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_name16S <- new_names

Bar plot agglomerated by order-facetted by fire factor

data16S <- sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  filter(Abundance > 0.05) %>%
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = Sample_name16S, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Phylum"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Fire_Mod), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning in geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_name16S", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

ggsave("Figures/16S/relabundance_plot_relabund_phylum_wildfire_samples.pdf", abund_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Let’s summarize with boxplot

# Assuming data16S is already prepared as in your script
data16S <- sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  filter(Abundance > 0.05) %>%
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

# Create the plot with both boxplot and jitter
combined_plot <- ggplot(data16S, aes(x = Fire_Mod, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +  # Boxplot with no outliers and some transparency
  geom_jitter(color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) +  # Jittered black dots
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option="H") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.position = "none",  # Remove the legend
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Phylum, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 40)

combined_plot

Let’s look top taxa at order level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Order")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/16S/top40_order_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_name16S", fill="Order") + facet_wrap(~Fire_Mod, scales="free_x")
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the phyloseq package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/joey711/phyloseq/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at family level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Family")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/16S/top40_family_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_name16S", fill="Family") + facet_wrap(~Fire_Mod, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at genus level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Genus")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/16S/top40_genus_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_name16S", fill="Genus") + facet_wrap(~Fire_Mod, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s make a table of top taxa.

data <-
  sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Phylum") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed <- data %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom'. You can override
## using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_phylum_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data1 <-
  sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed1 <- data1 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class',
## 'Order', 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed1, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_genus_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data2 <-
  sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed2 <- data2 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class',
## 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed2, file="Results/16S/mostabundanttaxa_species_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Let’s look at spacial placement of different plots

pdf("Figures/16S/location_of_plots_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)
map_plot <- ggplot(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_text(aes(color = Fire_Mod, label = Sample_name16S), size = 2.5, 
            check_overlap = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", title = "Sampling Locations") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20), 
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
print(map_plot)
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s normalize our data in preparation for beta diversity analyses

sdt = data.table::data.table(as(sample_data(sod_5asv.ps), "data.frame"),
                 TotalReads = sample_sums(sod_5asv.ps), keep.rownames = TRUE)
min_reads<-min(sdt$TotalReads)

rarefied.ps <- rarefy_even_depth(sod_5asv.ps, sample.size = min_reads, rngseed = 100)
## `set.seed(100)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(100); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 155OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## ...
strat_rel.ps<-transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(x) x / sum(x))
strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod<-as.factor(strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod)
strat_rel.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR<-as.factor(strat_rel.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR)
strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP<-as.factor(strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP)

Alpha diversity-use non-normalized or pruned data

Let’s start with t-test as point of comparison with anova analysis

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")))

factors <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")
ttest_list <- list()

for (factor_name in factors) {
  ttest <- data.table(alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric())
  
  for (alphad in c("InvSimpson", "Shannon", "Observed")) {
    ttest_res <- t.test(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad], var.equal = TRUE)
    p_value <- ttest_res$p.value
    ttest <- rbind(ttest, data.table(alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value))
  }
  
  ttest_list[[factor_name]] <- ttest
}

for (factor_name in factors) {
  cat(paste("T-Test Results for Factor:", factor_name), "\n")
  print(knitr::kable(ttest_list[[factor_name]], format = "markdown"))
  cat("\n")
}
## T-Test Results for Factor: Treatuntreat_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.0070186|
## |Shannon     | 0.0008969|
## |Observed    | 0.0001908|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: Lineage_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.3843064|
## |Shannon     | 0.8022624|
## |Observed    | 0.6918476|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: SoilBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.7485737|
## |Shannon     | 0.8976448|
## |Observed    | 0.5809477|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: VegBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.3315915|
## |Shannon     | 0.3179846|
## |Observed    | 0.1845573|

Let’s now proceed with ANOVA analyses and then compile our alpha diversity stats

set.seed(1)
anova_list <- list()

AD <- estimate_richness(sod.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")))

factors2 <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

plotted_factors <- character()

final_anova_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())

for (factor_name in factors2) {
  anova <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())
  
  for (alphad in c("Observed","InvSimpson", "Shannon")) {
    anova_res <- aov(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad])
    anova_summary <- summary(anova_res)
    p_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    df_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[1]
    df_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[2]
    ss_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[1]
    ss_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[2]
    ms_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[1]
    ms_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[2]
    F_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$"F value"[1]
    
    anova <- rbind(anova, data.table(factor_name = factor_name, alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value, df_between = df_between, df_within = df_within, ss_between = ss_between, ss_within = ss_within, ms_between = ms_between, ms_within = ms_within, F_value = F_value))
  }
  
  final_anova_table <- rbind(final_anova_table, anova)
}

print(knitr::kable(final_anova_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name     |alpha_index |    pvalue| df_between| df_within|   ss_between|    ss_within|   ms_between|    ms_within|   F_value|
## |:---------------|:-----------|---------:|----------:|---------:|------------:|------------:|------------:|------------:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    | 0.0001908|          1|        96| 1.114781e+05| 7.105163e+05| 1.114781e+05| 7401.2117925| 15.062138|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |InvSimpson  | 0.0070186|          1|        96| 7.726436e+03| 9.771152e+04| 7.726436e+03| 1017.8282982|  7.591100|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Shannon     | 0.0008969|          1|        96| 8.872492e-01| 7.247980e+00| 8.872492e-01|    0.0754998| 11.751678|
## |Fire_Mod        |Observed    | 0.0007967|          2|        95| 1.146463e+05| 7.073482e+05| 5.732313e+04| 7445.7700829|  7.698750|
## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  | 0.0064132|          2|        95| 1.063321e+04| 9.480474e+04| 5.316606e+03|  997.9446514|  5.327556|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     | 0.0020106|          2|        95| 9.968808e-01| 7.138348e+00| 4.984404e-01|    0.0751405|  6.633445|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Observed    | 0.0001102|          4|        93| 1.805157e+05| 6.414787e+05| 4.512893e+04| 6897.6201485|  6.542682|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |InvSimpson  | 0.0054243|          4|        93| 1.524311e+04| 9.019484e+04| 3.810779e+03|  969.8369703|  3.929298|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     | 0.0006920|          4|        93| 1.508999e+00| 6.626229e+00| 3.772498e-01|    0.0712498|  5.294750|
write.csv(final_anova_table, "Results/16S/final_anova_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s make faceted boxplots for our alpha diversity analyses

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_5asv.ps)[rownames(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

plots <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  p <- plot_richness(sod.ps, x = variable, measures = c("Shannon", "InvSimpson", "Observed")) + 
    geom_boxplot()
  plots[[variable]] <- p
}

plots_arrange <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  plots[["Treatuntreat_EP"]], plots[["Fire_Mod"]], plots[["YrSinceTrt_BR"]],ncol=1)

plots_arrange_with_text <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_plot(plots_arrange) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Treatuntreat_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Fire_Mod", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("YrSinceTrt_BR", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) 
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
ggsave(plots_arrange_with_text, path = "Figures/16S", filename = "alpha_diversity_each_factor_wildfire_samples.pdf", 
       width = 16, height = 24)

Let’s perform TUKEY HSD tests

set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

tukey_results <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~ ."))
    anova_res <- aov(formula, data = data_subset)
    tukey_result <- HSD.test(anova_res, variable)
    print(tukey_result)
  }
}
## $statistics
##    MSerror Df     Mean       CV
##   7401.212 96 320.4694 26.84509

## untreated 283.8667      b

Let’s visualize alpha diversity results with rarefaction curve

sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(sod.ps))
otu_data <- as(otu_table(sod.ps), "matrix")

sample_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sample_data$Fire_Mod, levels = c("Untreated", "Treated", "Wildfire"))

colors <- ifelse(sample_data$Fire_Mod == "Untreated", "blue",
                 ifelse(sample_data$Fire_Mod == "Treated", "red", "green"))

raremax <- min(rowSums(otu_data))

pdf("Figures/16S/rarefaction_curve_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)
rarecurve(otu_data, step = 10, sample = raremax, col = colors, cex = 0.6, label = FALSE, xlab = "Sequencing Depth", ylab = "Number of ASVs")
legend("topright", legend = levels(sample_data$Fire_Mod), col = c("blue", "red", "green"), lty = 1, title = "Treatuntreat_EP")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s also perform Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn’s Post-hoc tests in case our data violates key ANOVA assumptions

set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

final_kw_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                              chi_squared = numeric(), df = numeric(), pvalue = numeric())

final_dunn_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                                Comparison = character(), Z = numeric(),
                                P.unadj = numeric(), P.adj = numeric())

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~", variable))

    cat("\n=====", measure, "by", variable, "=====\n")
    kw_res <- kruskal.test(formula, data = data_subset)
    print(kw_res)

    final_kw_table <- rbind(final_kw_table, data.table(
      factor_name = variable, alpha_index = measure,
      chi_squared = unname(kw_res$statistic), df = unname(kw_res$parameter),
      pvalue = kw_res$p.value
    ))

    n_groups <- nlevels(droplevels(data_subset[[variable]]))
    if (n_groups == 2) {
  
      cat("(2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)\n")
    } else {
      dunn_res <- FSA::dunnTest(formula, data = data_subset, method = "bh")
      print(dunn_res)

      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_res$res
      dunn_table_sub$factor_name <- variable
      dunn_table_sub$alpha_index <- measure
      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_table_sub[, c("factor_name", "alpha_index", "Comparison", "Z", "P.unadj", "P.adj")]

      final_dunn_table <- rbind(final_dunn_table, dunn_table_sub)
    }
  }
}
## 
## ===== Observed by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 11.324, df = 1, p-value = 0.0007653
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 6.8266, df = 1, p-value = 0.008981
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 8.5641, df = 1, p-value = 0.003428
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Observed by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 11.485, df = 2, p-value = 0.003206
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 11.4852, df = 2, p-value = 0
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   3.269084
##          │     0.0032*
##          │
## Wildfire │   0.401924  -1.927874
##          │     0.6877     0.0808 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison          Z     P.unadj       P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated  3.2690845 0.001078961 0.003236882
## 2   Treated - Wildfire  0.4019242 0.687739820 0.687739820
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire -1.9278741 0.053870791 0.080806186
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 10.248, df = 2, p-value = 0.005952
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 10.248, df = 2, p-value = 0.01
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   3.116683
##          │     0.0055*
##          │
## Wildfire │   1.849707  -0.344949
##          │     0.0965     0.7301 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison          Z     P.unadj       P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated  3.1166834 0.001828979 0.005486937
## 2   Treated - Wildfire  1.8497080 0.064355650 0.096533476
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire -0.3449493 0.730132506 0.730132506
## 
## ===== Shannon by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 9.4405, df = 2, p-value = 0.008913
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 9.4405, df = 2, p-value = 0.01
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   3.063648
##          │     0.0066*
##          │
## Wildfire │   0.936121  -1.237147
##          │     0.3492     0.3240 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison          Z     P.unadj       P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated  3.0636486 0.002186556 0.006559667
## 2   Treated - Wildfire  0.9361213 0.349210712 0.349210712
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire -1.2371475 0.216032317 0.324048475
## 
## ===== Observed by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 15.736, df = 4, p-value = 0.003395
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 15.736, df = 4, p-value = 0
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     10to12       1to2       3to4       5to8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
##     1to2 │  -1.413652
##          │     0.3149 
##          │
##     3to4 │  -1.249306  -0.133687
##          │     0.3526     0.8936 
##          │
##     5to8 │  -2.094514  -1.233020  -0.874873
##          │     0.1207     0.3108     0.4771 
##          │
## untreate │   0.209264   2.878230   1.906723   3.046786
##          │     0.9269     0.0200*    0.1414     0.0231*
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##            Comparison          Z     P.unadj      P.adj
## 1       10to12 - 1to2 -1.4136529 0.157463846 0.31492769
## 2       10to12 - 3to4 -1.2493061 0.211553151 0.35258858
## 3         1to2 - 3to4 -0.1336877 0.893649558 0.89364956
## 4       10to12 - 5to8 -2.0945141 0.036214208 0.12071403
## 5         1to2 - 5to8 -1.2330201 0.217568250 0.31081179
## 6         3to4 - 5to8 -0.8748734 0.381642775 0.47705347
## 7  10to12 - untreated  0.2092643 0.834241937 0.92693549
## 8    1to2 - untreated  2.8782307 0.003999125 0.01999563
## 9    3to4 - untreated  1.9067238 0.056556363 0.14139091
## 10   5to8 - untreated  3.0467860 0.002313023 0.02313023
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 9.2886, df = 4, p-value = 0.05428
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 9.2886, df = 4, p-value = 0.05
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     10to12       1to2       3to4       5to8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
##     1to2 │  -0.156399
##          │     0.8757 
##          │
##     3to4 │  -0.806941  -0.884576
##          │     0.5246     0.6273 
##          │
##     5to8 │  -1.155200  -1.337545  -0.360482
##          │     0.4960     0.4526     0.7983 
##          │
## untreate │   0.834816   1.717566   1.972442   2.442192
##          │     0.5769     0.2863     0.2428     0.1460 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##            Comparison          Z    P.unadj     P.adj
## 1       10to12 - 1to2 -0.1563996 0.87571808 0.8757181
## 2       10to12 - 3to4 -0.8069414 0.41970025 0.5246253
## 3         1to2 - 3to4 -0.8845769 0.37638489 0.6273082
## 4       10to12 - 5to8 -1.1552003 0.24800841 0.4960168
## 5         1to2 - 5to8 -1.3375450 0.18104480 0.4526120
## 6         3to4 - 5to8 -0.3604822 0.71848655 0.7983184
## 7  10to12 - untreated  0.8348161 0.40382127 0.5768875
## 8    1to2 - untreated  1.7175662 0.08587577 0.2862526
## 9    3to4 - untreated  1.9724425 0.04855911 0.2427956
## 10   5to8 - untreated  2.4421929 0.01459834 0.1459834
## 
## ===== Shannon by YrSinceTrt_BR =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by YrSinceTrt_BR
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 14.051, df = 4, p-value = 0.007134
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 14.051, df = 4, p-value = 0.01
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │     10to12       1to2       3to4       5to8
## ─────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────
##     1to2 │  -0.967921
##          │     0.4758 
##          │
##     3to4 │  -1.454921  -0.871319
##          │     0.2428     0.4795 
##          │
##     5to8 │  -2.142945  -1.766207  -0.712172
##          │     0.1071     0.1547     0.5293 
##          │
## untreate │   0.202246   2.072353   2.176637   3.104681
##          │     0.8397     0.0956     0.1475     0.0190*
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##            Comparison          Z     P.unadj      P.adj
## 1       10to12 - 1to2 -0.9679218 0.333083422 0.47583346
## 2       10to12 - 3to4 -1.4549214 0.145691026 0.24281838
## 3         1to2 - 3to4 -0.8713193 0.383579863 0.47947483
## 4       10to12 - 5to8 -2.1429451 0.032117507 0.10705836
## 5         1to2 - 5to8 -1.7662076 0.077361031 0.15472206
## 6         3to4 - 5to8 -0.7121722 0.476358151 0.52928683
## 7  10to12 - untreated  0.2022464 0.839724081 0.83972408
## 8    1to2 - untreated  2.0723530 0.038232529 0.09558132
## 9    3to4 - untreated  2.1766376 0.029507620 0.14753810
## 10   5to8 - untreated  3.1046811 0.001904843 0.01904843
print(knitr::kable(final_kw_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name     |alpha_index | chi_squared| df|    pvalue|
## |:---------------|:-----------|-----------:|--:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    |   11.323638|  1| 0.0007653|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |InvSimpson  |    6.826618|  1| 0.0089809|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Shannon     |    8.564145|  1| 0.0034285|
## |Fire_Mod        |Observed    |   11.485181|  2| 0.0032065|
## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  |   10.248038|  2| 0.0059521|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     |    9.440468|  2| 0.0089131|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Observed    |   15.736018|  4| 0.0033946|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |InvSimpson  |    9.288618|  4| 0.0542767|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     |   14.051023|  4| 0.0071340|
write.csv(final_kw_table, "Results/16S/final_kruskalwallis_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

print(knitr::kable(final_dunn_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name   |alpha_index |Comparison           |          Z|   P.unadj|     P.adj|
## |:-------------|:-----------|:--------------------|----------:|---------:|---------:|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Treated - Untreated  |  3.2690845| 0.0010790| 0.0032369|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Treated - Wildfire   |  0.4019242| 0.6877398| 0.6877398|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Untreated - Wildfire | -1.9278741| 0.0538708| 0.0808062|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Treated - Untreated  |  3.1166834| 0.0018290| 0.0054869|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Treated - Wildfire   |  1.8497080| 0.0643557| 0.0965335|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Untreated - Wildfire | -0.3449493| 0.7301325| 0.7301325|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Treated - Untreated  |  3.0636486| 0.0021866| 0.0065597|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Treated - Wildfire   |  0.9361213| 0.3492107| 0.3492107|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Untreated - Wildfire | -1.2371475| 0.2160323| 0.3240485|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 1to2        | -1.4136529| 0.1574638| 0.3149277|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 3to4        | -1.2493061| 0.2115532| 0.3525886|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - 3to4          | -0.1336877| 0.8936496| 0.8936496|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 5to8        | -2.0945141| 0.0362142| 0.1207140|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - 5to8          | -1.2330201| 0.2175683| 0.3108118|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |3to4 - 5to8          | -0.8748734| 0.3816428| 0.4770535|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - untreated   |  0.2092643| 0.8342419| 0.9269355|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - untreated     |  2.8782307| 0.0039991| 0.0199956|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |3to4 - untreated     |  1.9067238| 0.0565564| 0.1413909|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |5to8 - untreated     |  3.0467860| 0.0023130| 0.0231302|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 1to2        | -0.1563996| 0.8757181| 0.8757181|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 3to4        | -0.8069414| 0.4197003| 0.5246253|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - 3to4          | -0.8845769| 0.3763849| 0.6273082|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 5to8        | -1.1552003| 0.2480084| 0.4960168|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - 5to8          | -1.3375450| 0.1810448| 0.4526120|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |3to4 - 5to8          | -0.3604822| 0.7184865| 0.7983184|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - untreated   |  0.8348161| 0.4038213| 0.5768875|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - untreated     |  1.7175662| 0.0858758| 0.2862526|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |3to4 - untreated     |  1.9724425| 0.0485591| 0.2427956|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |5to8 - untreated     |  2.4421929| 0.0145983| 0.1459834|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 1to2        | -0.9679218| 0.3330834| 0.4758335|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 3to4        | -1.4549214| 0.1456910| 0.2428184|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - 3to4          | -0.8713193| 0.3835799| 0.4794748|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 5to8        | -2.1429451| 0.0321175| 0.1070584|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - 5to8          | -1.7662076| 0.0773610| 0.1547221|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |3to4 - 5to8          | -0.7121722| 0.4763582| 0.5292868|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - untreated   |  0.2022464| 0.8397241| 0.8397241|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - untreated     |  2.0723530| 0.0382325| 0.0955813|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |3to4 - untreated     |  2.1766376| 0.0295076| 0.1475381|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |5to8 - untreated     |  3.1046811| 0.0019048| 0.0190484|
write.csv(final_dunn_table, "Results/16S/final_dunns_posthoc_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Beta diversity-PERMANOVA analyses-ASV counts transformed (rel. abundance)

set.seed(1)
mapping <- as.data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

mapping2=mapping[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

ASV_tables_bray <- phyloseq::distance(strat_rel.ps, method = "bray")

df_metadata <- data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

df_metadata<-df_metadata[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

pvals <- sapply(colnames(mapping2), function(variable){
  form1<-as.formula(paste("ASV_tables_bray", variable, sep="~"))
  
  res <- adonis2(form1, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)
  
  # extract pvalue from adonis output
  pval <- res$`Pr(>F)`[[1]] 
  return(pval)
})

#Adjust p_values with fdr correction
pval_adj <- p.adjust(pvals, method = "fdr")
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis <- pval_adj[pval_adj < 0.05]
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis
## Treatuntreat_EP        Fire_Mod   YrSinceTrt_BR 
##           3e-04           3e-04           8e-04
print(pval_adj)
## Treatuntreat_EP        Fire_Mod   YrSinceTrt_BR 
##           3e-04           3e-04           8e-04

Compile PERMANOVA stats

set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray~Fire_Mod, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999, by="terms")
res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]

res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)

res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Fire_Mod"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##    Factor Df SumOfSqs         R2        F  pr_f FDR_adj_p Bonferroni_adj_p
##  Fire_Mod  2 1.440769 0.05783097 2.915582 1e-04     1e-04            1e-04
##  P_Value_BetaDispersion
##                   0.015
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/16S/PERMANOVA_with_betadisper_maineffects_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s now examine beta diversity through ordination plots

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS", 
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.1604368 
## Run 1 stress 0.1716729 
## Run 2 stress 0.1612928 
## Run 3 stress 0.1607819 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01560619  max resid 0.1123022 
## Run 4 stress 0.1610044 
## Run 5 stress 0.1604647 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002323802  max resid 0.01999985 
## Run 6 stress 0.1610044 
## Run 7 stress 0.1622277 
## Run 8 stress 0.1607371 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01719852  max resid 0.1120925 
## Run 9 stress 0.1612817 
## Run 10 stress 0.1615491 
## Run 11 stress 0.1609481 
## Run 12 stress 0.1903654 
## Run 13 stress 0.1613659 
## Run 14 stress 0.1604646 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002279422  max resid 0.01955908 
## Run 15 stress 0.1615656 
## Run 16 stress 0.1613251 
## Run 17 stress 0.1612624 
## Run 18 stress 0.1616372 
## Run 19 stress 0.1605033 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.005692339  max resid 0.05284277 
## Run 20 stress 0.1604806 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002754954  max resid 0.02022705 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: stress ratio > sratmax
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         color= "Treatuntreat_EP") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  labs(color = "Factor") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))

plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

write.csv(ps_NMDS[["points"]], file="Results/16S/16S_NMDS_coordinates_persample_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot (rel abundance dataset), with Fire_Mod factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.1604368 
## Run 1 stress 0.1716729 
## Run 2 stress 0.1612928 
## Run 3 stress 0.1607819 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01560619  max resid 0.1123022 
## Run 4 stress 0.1610044 
## Run 5 stress 0.1604647 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002323802  max resid 0.01999985 
## Run 6 stress 0.1610044 
## Run 7 stress 0.1622277 
## Run 8 stress 0.1607371 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01719852  max resid 0.1120925 
## Run 9 stress 0.1612817 
## Run 10 stress 0.1615491 
## Run 11 stress 0.1609481 
## Run 12 stress 0.1903654 
## Run 13 stress 0.1613659 
## Run 14 stress 0.1604646 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002279422  max resid 0.01955908 
## Run 15 stress 0.1615656 
## Run 16 stress 0.1613251 
## Run 17 stress 0.1612624 
## Run 18 stress 0.1616372 
## Run 19 stress 0.1605033 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.005692339  max resid 0.05284277 
## Run 20 stress 0.1604806 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002754954  max resid 0.02022705 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: stress ratio > sratmax
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "Fire_Mod") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot rel abundance-with YrSinceTrt_BR factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.1604368 
## Run 1 stress 0.1716729 
## Run 2 stress 0.1612928 
## Run 3 stress 0.1607819 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01560619  max resid 0.1123022 
## Run 4 stress 0.1610044 
## Run 5 stress 0.1604647 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002323802  max resid 0.01999985 
## Run 6 stress 0.1610044 
## Run 7 stress 0.1622277 
## Run 8 stress 0.1607371 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.01719852  max resid 0.1120925 
## Run 9 stress 0.1612817 
## Run 10 stress 0.1615491 
## Run 11 stress 0.1609481 
## Run 12 stress 0.1903654 
## Run 13 stress 0.1613659 
## Run 14 stress 0.1604646 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002279422  max resid 0.01955908 
## Run 15 stress 0.1615656 
## Run 16 stress 0.1613251 
## Run 17 stress 0.1612624 
## Run 18 stress 0.1616372 
## Run 19 stress 0.1605033 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.005692339  max resid 0.05284277 
## Run 20 stress 0.1604806 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.002754954  max resid 0.02022705 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##     20: stress ratio > sratmax
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "YrSinceTrt_BR") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses
